reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) - Batch of 2026
reviews_list.review_title: Not satisfied with faculty's knowledge and campus infrastructure,but overall it has value for money.

review_content.Placements: Approximately 50–60% were placed in my course, with the highest package being 6 LPA and the lowest being 4 LPA. I forgot the names of the companies, but approximately 60% got internships at TCS, and roles like customer support executive and marketing team were offered in my course.
review_content.Infrastructure: There is Wi-Fi in our college, and there are two huge computer labs with 30 computers. However, our college does not offer hostel facilities as it is only a building, so there are no hostels or any other amenities.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Legislative Law (L.L.B.) - Batch of 2022
reviews_list.review_title: All the necessary facilities are available here for studies.

review_content.Placements: Many students got selected for internships for accounting advocates, and they offer a good amount of stipend. Nearly 40% of the student from our course got internships. Our course is professional, so no other roles are offered other than the internship.
review_content.Infrastructure: Our college looks small from the front side, but in the backside, it has a huge space. Our college has a library, classroom and a projector room for our department. Wi-Fi facility is not available here, and a computer lab is also not available. There is no air-conditioning facility in rooms, and food facility is also not available here. There are no medical facility and sports equipment to play.

reviews_list.review_title: My placement was in the year 2024.

review_content.Placements: In my course, around 85% of students are successfully placed. In my course, the highest package is 12 LPA, and the lowest package is 3 LPA. reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) - Batch of 2031
reviews_list.review_title: Quite satisfied with this college and its good here.

review_content.Placements: Approximate 50-60% were placed from my course and the lowest package was about 2,00,000 p.a. and highest was 3,60,000 p.a. Top recruiting companies were TCS and about 70% students got internship in CA firms or in TCS and roles mostly offered are related managerial skills.
review_content.Infrastructure: College's library and classrooms are quite good and there is not any kind of wi-fi network available for students in college campus and about hostel there is no facility providing hostel services by college and there is good facility for sports and games.

reviews_list.review_title: It has good academics, nice events, and limited placements, but no hostel.

review_content.Placements: In our college, placement opportunities are quite limited. Only a low percentage of students get placed in college. Most of them have to look for jobs on their own. As far as I know, there are no regular placement drives or big companies coming for recruitment. Internships are not commonly offered through the college. However, some students manage to get internships on their own in small firms or local businesses. The focus here is more on academics rather than placements.
review_content.Infrastructure: Grace College has a clean and well-maintained campus. Classrooms are spacious and comfortable for regular lectures. Wi-Fi is available in some areas of the campus and the library has a decent collection of academic books. Labs and other basic facilities required for the course are available and function well. However, the college does not have its own hostel. Students usually stay in private hostels or PGs nearby. There is no mess or canteen inside the college, so most students depend on nearby food outlets. Medical and sports facilities are limited but basic support is provided when needed.

answer.text: No, After completing LLB you become a lawyer, however, you are still not an advocate and hence cannot practice law. To become an advocate and to practice law as a profession, you mandatorily need to clear the All India Bar Examination (AIBE). faqs.question: Can I become a lawyer after completing my LLB?

answer.text: No, you cannot become a lawyer without a Law degree. You mandatorily need to obtain degrees such as BA/BCom/BSc/BBA LLB or LLB in order to be a lawyer in India. To become a lawyer, one must first complete three-year or five-year LLB from a recognised university and possess degree certificate. Then, they must enrol themselves in local Bar Council of the concerned state and enrolled as an advocate. Within two years of the enrolment, they must appear and clear AIBE in order to be eligible to practise anywhere in India.

highlights.summary:
Maya Academy of Advanced Cinematics (MAAC) is the media and entertainment education vertical of Aptech Ltd, the leading skill development solution provider in the world. With a vast network of centres globally, MAAC has trained lakhs of students for making a successful career in the field of media and entertainment. MAAC students won many prestigious national and international awards including the Dadashaeb Phalke Best Animation Student Award 2018, ChitraBharati Film Festival Award 2018, Rolling Frames Award, FICCI BAF Award, Japanese International Film Festival Award and Golden Cursor Award. Mumbai centres of MAAC are among the best learning centres worldwide for media and entertainment courses. It offers best-in-class education in animation, visual effects, game designing, filmmaking, multimedia, graphic design, augmented and virtual reality (AR/VR), web designing, broadcast and much more.

The academy features well-equipped classrooms, top-notch animation tools, the latest software and state-of-the-art studios promoting an advanced learning system. It has highly-skilled faculty members who also possess significant experience in the field. Students at MAAC get a hands-on learning experience in a studio-like environment and deal with industry-specific software. Further, students tend to develop an insight into the dynamics of real-world work environments through industry workshops and studio visits.

Admission to various courses at MAAC Mumbai is lateral in nature. The basic eligibility for the majority of courses is class XII. The candidates need to apply online through the official website of the institute.

For the detailed admission process, Click Here

MAAC has its credibility in the media and entertainment industry. It maintains a dedicated placement cell to facilitate the placement process of the students. Students get recruited in various job roles such as 3D modeller, art director, film & video editor, VFX artist, graphic designer, colour key artist, UI designer, website developer, concept artist, gaming experts, flash animator, cartoonist, character animator, digital painter, compositing artist, stop motion animator and character rigger.
